title: PG&E Sets Dates for Quarterly Stock Dividends
author: Prnewswire
description: PG&E Corporation declared its second quarter 2026 regular cash dividend of $0.05 per share on common stock, payable July 15, 2026. The company also declared preferred stock dividends across eight series, payable August 15, 2026. PG&E serves 16 million Californians across Northern and Central California.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is maintaining regular dividend payments to shareholders, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to returning capital to investors. Consistent dividend declarations are generally viewed positively by income-focused investors and indicate operational confidence.

title: 10 S&P 500 Stocks Showing Strong Upside Despite Market at Record Highs
author: David Wagner
description: The S&P 500 reached a new record at 7,137.90, driven by reduced Iran tensions and strong earnings with 88% of companies beating profit estimates. While the market appears expensive at a forward P/E of 20x, selective stock picking remains important. Three highlighted undervalued stocks with significant upside potential are Zebra Technologies, PG&E, and Fair Isaac, each showing 25-58% undervaluation with analyst upside of 35-75%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company shows strong Q1 2026 earnings expectations (~10% YoY growth) and confident full-year guidance ($17.70-$18.30). Benefits from growing automation and e-commerce trends. Identified as undervalued with significant upside potential.

Utility stock gaining attention as stable investment with expected 10% YoY earnings growth. Benefits from rising power demand from AI and data centers with projects exceeding 9.5 gigawatts. Identified as undervalued with upside potential.

title: US Utility Spending To Hit $1.4 Trillion By 2030 As AI, Data Centers Drive Demand— Rate Hikes Ahead?
author: Namrata Sen
description: U.S. investor-owned utilities plan to spend $1.4 trillion on capital projects through 2030, a 21% increase driven primarily by AI and data center expansion. This surge in spending is expected to lead to future rate increase requests, with utilities already seeking $31 billion in rate hikes in 2025 alone. The top 5 utilities account for over half of planned capital expenditures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Highest capital expenditure plans at $102.8 billion through 2030, positioning the company to benefit from infrastructure investment boom driven by AI and data center demand.

title: Billionaire Investor Reshuffles Utility Bets, Buys Constellation Energy And Trims PG&E
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Hedge fund billionaire Daniel Loeb's Third Point LLC opened a new position in Constellation Energy with 475,000 shares while reducing its PG&E stake from 50.1 million to 34.3 million shares. Constellation Energy announced a partnership with Nvidia to build AI data centers and reported strong Q4 earnings, while PG&E reduced electric rates for the fifth time since January 2024.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q4 earnings beat ($2.30 EPS vs $2.23 consensus), major Nvidia partnership for AI data centers, significant power agreements with CyrusOne, and 27.14% year-over-year stock performance. Billionaire investor opening new position signals confidence.

Mixed earnings results with EPS meeting consensus but revenue missing expectations. Positive rate cuts implemented, but significant stake reduction by major investor and underperformance versus sector peers (-3.99 percentage points) indicate cautious outlook.

title: Investor Makes $30 Million Bet on Another Bitcoin Miner Pivoting Toward AI Data Centers
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Covalis (Gibraltar) Ltd invested $30.29 million in Core Scientific, acquiring 2,080,550 shares in Q4 2025. The investment reflects Core Scientific's strategic pivot from bitcoin mining toward AI data centers and high-density colocation services, with the company scaling toward 1.5 gigawatt of leasable capacity. Colocation revenue surged to $65 million in 2025 from $24 million in 2024, positioning the company as an AI infrastructure play.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong institutional investment of $30 million, significant business pivot toward high-growth AI infrastructure, colocation revenue nearly tripled year-over-year, stock up 81% over past year, and positioned as a critical enabler in the emerging AI compute infrastructure market.
